title: Semantic module consolidation audit and compatibility-preserving merge/no-merge gate
how_implemented: No Lean refactor has been executed. V1.12 creates the active pre-substantive audit gate and records initial code-derived candidate clusters plus no-merge safeguards.
verification_evidence: Static repository facts: 287 Lean modules and zero missing internal import edges from repo_inventory; direct spot-checks found clone/schema families in BoundarySource substrate ledgers and Handoff input/output wrappers; long SM and Cayley-Dickson chains remain proof-stage candidates for keep/no-merge until a dedicated provenance-preservation proof exists.
implementation_notes: This phase is deliberately before 1.1.1.2. Its output is not a code merge, but a precise ledger deciding whether a later bounded Lean refactor phase is warranted. The default is no merge unless semantic equivalence, natural-layer placement, facade compatibility, and regression evidence are explicit.
proof_strategy: Refactor-governance proof by finite inventory plus targeted code inspection: identify clusters, classify safe/unsafe consolidation, require compatibility facades and regression metrics before any Lean file is moved or merged.
build_result: No Lean build run; no Lean source changed. Python table generation and PDF compilation only.
forbidden_patterns_checked: no Lean source move in the audit phase; no merge by filename similarity alone; no deletion of old import surfaces without facade migration; no merging proof-stage chains that encode derivation/provenance order; no new semantic truth layer from the audit ledger
preparation_workflow: Semantic module consolidation and compatibility-preserving refactor workflow.
source_materials: repo_inventory/raw_export/modules.json; repo_inventory/raw_export/import_edges.json; repo_inventory/raw_export/symbol_reference_summary.json; BoundarySource substrate candidate modules; Handoff input/output wrapper modules; Smoke/SM generated proof-chain modules; Structural/CayleyDickson/S6 proof-chain modules; Arithmetic/Notation.lean size/role observation
extracted_theory_objects: safe-merge candidate cluster; facade-preserve candidate cluster; proof-stage no-merge cluster; module compactness regression metric; natural-layer merge placement rule
source_to_cnna_mapping: This is not a mathematical CNNA object. It maps repository structure to refactor governance: only semantically identical representation layers may be consolidated; proof-producing layers remain split unless their proof/provenance information is preserved by a better canonical module.
dependency_depth_audit: Prerequisites closed: module manifest LEG10, reuse/generator policy LEG11, proofbook infrastructure LEG12, declaration consumption map LEG5, phase-object policy LEG21, and refactor regression protocol GNG03. Blocking item: a per-cluster ledger is not yet closed, so 1.1.1.2 is now orange rather than active.
phase_split_decision: Create one active audit gate 1.1.1.1.5 now. Only if the ledger finds safe changes should it open bounded execution subphases, e.g. Handoff feedback wrappers, BoundarySource candidate ledgers, or notation-facade normalization. Do not split by every file family yet.
positive_path_plan: Fill LEG36 with candidate clusters: high-confidence consolidate/facade families, medium-risk schema abstractions, explicit no-merge proof-stage families, and deferred notation-facade normalization. Then either close with no Lean changes or open one small refactor execution phase with old-module facades and local build evidence.
proof_rehearsal: For each candidate cluster prove/check: same semantic operation, same or explicitly mapped public declarations, no consumer lost, import direction preserved or improved, natural generating layer chosen, old imports kept through facades, full local build green, and statement/provenance granularity not lost.
expected_tactics: If later Lean facades are created, expect explicit abbrev/def wrappers, namespace-preserving aliases, extensionality reuse where existing, and rfl proofs. Avoid tactic-heavy rewrites; no @[simp] promotion in operational path.
expected_mathlib_imports: No new mathlib imports expected for audit. Any later facade/refactor should reuse existing imports; new imports require Lean 4.28.0 verification.
exec_complex_plan: No ExecComplex changes in the audit. If Arithmetic/Notation or operational modules are touched later, preserve all concrete executable witnesses and rational-boundary policy.
bridge_gap_status: Current bridge gap is governance-level: exact consolidation candidates are not yet fully ledgered. No mathematical bridge is missing. Do not patch consumers; place any new facade at the natural source layer of the duplicate/schema family.
natural_backjump_or_refactor_layer: BoundarySource candidate ledgers belong under Arithmetic/BoundarySource; Handoff input/output wrapper consolidation belongs under Handoff/Inputs or Outputs; notation normalization belongs at notation facade boundaries; proof-stage chains stay in their proof-stage folders unless a canonical proof module is naturally introduced.
implementation_prompt_contract: Before writing any new substantive Lean code, complete the LEG36 audit. no_go_guardrails: GLOBAL-INHERITED: all global_no_go_guardrails apply, especially GNG03, GNG21, GNG23-GNG26.; no semantic merge without natural-layer placement; no deletion of compatibility imports in the first refactor pass; no proof-stage chain merge by naming similarity; no code slimming that reduces statement/provenance auditability

artifact_packaging_rule: Return a complete zip with updated YAML, generated tables/exports, report, and PDF. Do not claim Lean build success unless actually run or user-reported.

mathlib_complication_log: No mathlib complication in audit. Later code movement may expose import-minimization issues; resolve by preserving existing import surfaces first.
noncomputable_boundary_audit: No noncomputable introduced. Later operational refactor cannot introduce noncomputable/classical pressure into executable paths.
mathlib_mitigation_or_refactor_layer: If new imports are needed, prefer local facade modules or existing imported modules rather than widening mathlib dependency surface.
phase_type_specific_contract: Refactor phase: wrapper-first/facade-first, bounded migration package, import/declaration diff, consumer-breakage audit, and local full-build evidence before closeout.

exec_complex_boundary_policy: EXEC-2 remains unchanged. Consolidation may not move rational executable data behind noncomputable or analytic mirrors.
consumer_contract_direction: Audit flows from code producers/import surfaces to consumers. If a consumer needs a consolidated API, the facade must be created at the producer/natural source layer, not patched in the consumer.
governance_overhead_review: This gate is justified because concrete clone families exist, but it must remain compact: one ledger, no broad rewrite, no full code normalization before the active content path.
module_implementation_manifest: No Lean module added now. Potential future modules, if authorized: CNNA/PillarA/Handoff/Outputs/SectorExportAliases.lean; CNNA/PillarA/Handoff/Inputs/FeedbackInput.lean; CNNA/PillarA/Arithmetic/BoundarySource/CandidateLedgers.lean. Old modules remain as facades initially.
semantic_placement_decision: The audit belongs after declaration consumption classification and before blue-object content correction. This is the natural layer because it decides public/import surfaces before new semantic content is added.
reuse_and_duplication_audit: RD08 records the consolidation decision rule. Same-semantics wrappers may be unified; proof-stage modules must remain separated until a stronger canonical proof module preserves their role.
canonical_generator_policy: No generator change. If consolidation touches generated Smoke files later, it must use the existing canonical generated-source/main-path seams and must not create a second generator path.
module_compactness_rule: Slim modules only when statement/provenance auditability is preserved or improved. Small clone wrappers are candidates; long proof chains are not automatically candidates; a monolithic notation facade may require splitting rather than merging.

lean_mathlib_version_policy: Baseline is Lean 4.28.0/mathlib snapshot unless an explicit phase-boundary upgrade gate is opened. New imports must be verified against the chosen baseline; noncomputable/classical pressure must be logged and isolated.
exec_complex_boundary_policy: EXEC-2 likely relevant: algebraic irrational geometry/spectral/modular values are mirror-only unless a rational/integer invariant is separately proved for operational return.
consumer_contract_direction: Use minimal source objects: Path B consumes Phase 4.1 directly; Path A consumes D/order/lattice/modular prerequisites; bridges are explicit and late.
governance_overhead_review: Pending until first real refactor/code-change package closes; then record which governance gates prevented concrete errors and whether later phases can be flatter.
